title: Evangelibon artbook
wikitext:
[]: Evangelibon artbook             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Jul 09, 2013 4:39 pm http://avocado-slice.tumblr.com/post/54976266063/because-i-have-seen-the-comic-angel-kiss-going Released only a month after the series' premiere. Content: An interview with Anno, that I would really like to read, but there is no scan in the internet, in the last pages there are some genga (only four or five I think) and the rest of the book is the worst thing that has ever happened to Evangelion. This is basically hentai drawn by people that participated in the series (Kazuya Tsurumaki is credited, I think). I'm OK with the fan-service in the series, why not? In fact, I couldn't imagine Eva without it. I'm OK with some doujinshi that have been made, even in this book there seem to be some comics that aren't to be taken seriously. But the majority of the book: porn involving 14 year olds. That's it. Fuck the Japanese government for not taking any actions against this type of product, and fuck Gainax for releasing it and shitting all over their best anime in the process. And fuck Anno. Yes, I love Anno, but still, fuck him for not caring what they do with his characters. I don't care what any of the people that worked on this book do, their work for me is like the work of any other otaku on the internet. What bugs me is that Anno (apparently) didn't care. That's why I would like to read the interview. I don't know if this is related to the japaneses' way of thinking, but honestly, I don't care. If sex is is treated like something completely normal, awesome. But that has nothing to do with selling this kind of shit. I repeat, this is porn involving teenagers (I don't care if they are drawn), not made by fans, no, it is made by Gainax. This is kind of ruining Evangelion for me today. EDIT: Oh, and all that talk about making Rei a character that was supposed to shock otakus, this book shits on this too, by including a comic in which she is raped and she doesn't respond in any way. EDIT2: This artbook was made by Gainax staff, but was not published by Gainax.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 09, 2013 5:26 pm You must be new to this whole doujinshi thing .

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 09, 2013 5:33 pm Doesn't mean we have to like it just because the Japanese do, though.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Jul 09, 2013 6:48 pm Ornette, I already read that thread some time ago, but I didn't have any problem with it. The doujinshi that Anno participated in didn't seem like anything bad. I've always found this kind of thing, like Roshi, the character from Dragon Ball, funny. The doujinshi don't appeal to me in any way, but that's just my opinion.But this artbook takes it to a whole new level. So I agree with pwhodges. But it's not just that we don't have to like it, it is something that should be prohibited. At least for big companys like Gainax. You may call me extreme, but that's my honest opinion. I'm happy that I haven't seen anything like this from khara. Maybe Gainax just needed to do that for the money. Still awful.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 09, 2013 8:05 pm My point was further down in that thread where there's a list of all the doujin works that the people at GAINAX had worked on, the list is pretty extensive. It's one of the ways to get into the business, and like it or not, they all like to draw naked girls. And no, I'm not saying you should like this stuff, but I do think you're extreme reaction could be a result of not understanding the industry. Statements like: kuribo-04 wrote: I'm happy that I haven't seen anything like this from khara. Maybe Gainax just needed to do that for the money. Still awful.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Jul 09, 2013 8:50 pm Oh, sorry. I didn't understand you then. Well, I'm glad that Gainax doesn't publish stuff like this to make money. I think I remember that there was an official erotic artbook by Gainax, but as long as it doesn't contain sex with underage characters like this one I'm OK with it. That is actually the only complaint I have. It seems that many manga artists participate in these doujins, even famous ones like Anno. The problem was just that this one was too exteme, and it shocked me that even people that worked on such an important anime as Eva participated. Thinking that it was published by Gainax made it seem like something generally accepted, as if it were completely normal. Still, I know that there are mangas sold in Japan that include underage characters in sexual acts, and that the government doesn't take actions against it. And even if this artbook was only a doujinshi, it's autors are still..." so fucked up ".

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Jul 10, 2013 12:35 am Man, i can't believe that such petty bullshit brought me from my retreat, really ought to do something about that raeg. That said, i'm going to be honest and say that this topic and OP bother me *really* much. It's not even just from a demonization of sexuality and an unapologetic demostration of intolerance and arrogance, but i personally feel that getting so worked out over things like these and getting to the point of saying this should be prohibited vastly degrades and cheapens the importance of some other much more relevant issues. Then again, it is easier to go after silly things rather than fixing the real problem. Communication of my absolute disgust and comptent for OP and this thread aside, i admit i am somewhat confused. Specifically, what is it about all this (or maybe the combination) that gets OP so worked up. As far as i know the two that bothers him the most are that the official GAINAX people were the ones who made this and that it features "minors". I'm going to try to ignore OP's arrogance in implying he knows better than Anno and Japan (those wacky 17 years old) i am somewhat confused about why he is bothered about the fact the authors at GAINAX were also the authors of this (which admittedly seems quite a nasty doujin going by the description. Is it that he OP and people like him find them respectable artists or something like that? Or is it because those are GAINAX characters and they're making money out of it? Or do they hate that these artist like drawing naked girls? I'm sorry if i come accross as clueless, it's just that i really don't get why is that (the fact the authors at GAINAX made a doujin) a problem. But it seems that the fact the kids are underage is what bothers our silly OP. Which admitedly is something that could be kinda unpleasant... It is a complex matter. I feel that the fact these are fictional characters make any possible point of contention moot (it isn't as if most authors can make convincing teenagers anyways), so i'll try to account for that. Admittedly, the idea of exploiting vulnerable or manipulable people for some sort of gratification is certainly terrible, but... no, i'm sorry. These are fictional characters and i just can't pretend that an arbitrary age and body type set by the author makes that much of a magical difference. I was planning on writing a paragraph on the matter, but it probably wouldn't help anything. Most of anything i would just like to ask OP: What is it that bothers you so much about the fact these were 14 years old on the doujin?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 10, 2013 12:59 am Dream wrote: What is it that bothers you so much about the fact these were 14 years old on the doujin?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Jul 10, 2013 1:14 am pwhodges wrote: In many places that counts as child porn, which is widely considered the worst of all - not only morally but legally. And while we may understand that other societies have different standards, and may get along fine that way, that doesn't in itself give a reason to abandon our own standards.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Jul 10, 2013 9:28 am I really don't know why you are calling me silly, arrogant, etc. I've sometimes read posts from you and didn't expect that. Whatever I have written in this thread, I haven't insulted anybody on this forum, and while it may sound stupid, when somebody I don't know insults me, even if it is with a word like silly, which isn't actually that bad a word, I feel like shit. So maybe after this post I will just stop expressing my opinion on evageeks, something that I actually have the right to do. It really surprises me that somebody could disagree with me on this topic. It's true that I was swearing in my first post, and I apologize for that, but I was really angry. Still, I think you haven't read all of my posts, because some answers you ask were already responded. I'm less angry now, I THOUGHT that this artbook was published by Gainax. But Ornette explained to me that this book was just a doujinshi. From my previous post: "Thinking that it was published by Gainax made it seem like something generally accepted, as if it were completely normal." Gainax is an important company, so if Gainax had released this, everybody would know. And that would mean that nobody cared. THAT was what shocked me. You also ask why I think it is bad that Gainax staff participated. This is also from my first post: "I don't care what any of the people that worked on this book do, their work for me is like the work of any other otaku on the internet." That means that even if they are Gainax staff they aren't Anno, they aren't responsible for the characters. What was shocking me was that I thought the official company was releasing this. Now I'm quoting you: "Personally i can count many things (exploitation, slavery, hatred or any other such thing that tangibly and gravely destroys people's happiness or quality of life) as much worse than child porn." It's obvious that you aren't informed on the subject. I know people that have been sexually assaulted in some way when they were children, something that causes depression and low self-esteem the rest of your life. I don't care if this is fiction. Nobody makes comics about slavery as if it were something that is there to please people. That's why I think that releasing a comic in which a teenage girl is raped by 5 guys is something completely ugly. It's just wrong, and I'm not being arrogant. I respect different opinions, I said that I had no problem with the doujinshi and fan service community. But this book crosses the line. The response that pwhodges gave you is the logical response that I would give you, everybody that I know would give you, and I thought that everybody had the same opinion. I'm not as angry as I was in the beginning, thanks to Ornette I know that this is not related to Eva, Anno and Gainax in the way I thought. Also, I don't expect anything like this from Anno, since he would be contradicting himself. Again, with this I don't mean the fan service or doujinshi culture, I mean extreme cases like this one. Still, I know that in Japan this kind of thing is selled, and it is something that I will always hate about an otherwise very interesting country, whose way of thinking I love. Also, I'm not hating on normal Japanese people, but on the government on Japan, which is horrible, also because of other reasons (see how they hide from the population the whole dolphin hunting culture in the award-winning documentary The Cove, and people blame normal Japanese people even if it isn't their fault). I won't use the "It's just my opinion excuse", it's the opinion of almost everybody I'd say, at least to some extent. So I won't hide away from what I said. The only way this thread can make sense now is if we remember that this includes an Anno interview, so that we can read it someday.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 10, 2013 10:19 am No children were harmed in the making of that doujin; and all available evidence suggests that pornography is a substitute rather than a complement for the deed itself (whatever that may be) Diamond et al, abstract wrote: Pornography continues to be a contentious matter with those on the one side arguing it detrimental to society while others argue it is pleasurable to many and a feature of free speech. The advent of the Internet with the ready availability of sexually explicit materials thereon particularly has seemed to raise questions of its influence. Following the effects of a new law in the Czech Republic that allowed pornography to a society previously having forbidden it allowed us to monitor the change in sex related crime that followed the change. As found in all other countries in which the phenomenon has been studied, rape and other sex crimes did not increase. Of particular note is that this country, like Denmark and Japan, had a prolonged interval during which possession of child pornography was not illegal and, like those other countries, showed a significant decrease in the incidence of child sex abuse.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Jul 10, 2013 2:39 pm I must be a freaking masochist... Well, i'm not sure what to say. I'm sad to say this but it can't really say i'm sorry that you feel so bad at being called silly or such because in my perspective you earned it by calling the staff at Gainax a bunch of perverted otakus (which they admittedly kinda are) that ought to get fucked and implying you know better than a entire goverment or country, then protecting all this under the opinion badge. If you're going to act this way whenever someone gives you shit for an intolerant opinion then maybe you should indeed stop expressing your opinion on EGF. I'm sorry, but i just can't respect an opinion that isn't backed by arguments or facts and i feel that the arguments you have presented up to now are insufficient. I don't understand why the idea that someone might disagree with you (even in such a charged topic as this) is such a shocking concept, but anyways... I did read the entire thread before posting but i apologize if i missed something. The idea that Gainax would have released such erotic is certainly weird and kind of unpleasant, but on principle no different from a relatively obscure group doing the same thing. You could use a pragmatic argument on why the size and importance of Gainax makes a difference, but as far as i'm concerned that's completely separated from the legitimacy of such work. I'm going to be honest here, saying that by releasing this the staff and Gainax and Anno "aren't Gainax or Anno" is to me a very delusional/naive and disgusting mindset. Who are you to make a judgement on who or what Anno or Gainax is and/or represents? I wouldn't mind so much if you only said that this devalued their other works or that "I don't care what any of the people that worked on this book do, their work for me is like the work of any other otaku on the internet.", while i would point out that this seems to be a very unreasonable and inmature view, it seems to fall more into an emotional interpretation rather than intellectual judgement. But what bothers me is that some of what you wrote in there makes me think of a delusional that freaks out when they discover their idol isn't the way they fantasized. I would like that with this i'm not intending to call you stupid or delusional, and i am aware of how hostile it must sound, but i just can't think of a better way to explain my reasoning (a huge failure on my part!). Arguing that i am not aware of how sexual abuse affects children (or anyone, really) is i guess a not too unreasonable thing to believe, but i must inform you that you are mistaken (although i will admit i'm not very knowledgeable on the subject), and if you think that i am ok with real children being used for this or that in some other way i, or other people who hold a similar opinion, romanticize sexual exploitation (at least i have the feeling you might think this) you are very, *very* mistaken. No one is in favor of that. Also, there totally are works of fiction about slavery or other such things to please people, both to those who fantasize with being slavers or slaves. Again, not trying to come accross as condescending but i feel that you regarding the matter as way more simple than it actually is. Now, i don't know about this book anything more than the link you gave us. While there certainly is some really disgusting stuff (Gendo/Shinji comes to mind ), and while a "girl being by 5 [fat] guys" is something that is certainly really heartrending and terrible, i urge you to understand that there is a huge difference between this fictionalized or "sexy" stuff and the real thing. Again i would like to clarify i'm not implying you're not good at distinguishing reality from fiction. I'm referring to something completely different. In this forum i used to be a part of, there was this guy who had sexually unconventional tastes. One day, he posted in that forum's version of the FML thread saying he wondered why, even when he enjoyed doujinshi or other stuff where some guy invades a woman's house or keeps a bunch of women in captivity or something like that, when he saw equivalent scenes in films, books, news or other serious media, he was either heartbroken or repulsed by it. This guy would probably oppose real abuse as much as you or anyone. That said, i must admit i am surprised that you seem to be ok/uncensoring of young people's sexuality, since in my experience most people who hold these opinions are also pretty restrictive or censoring of teenager's exploration of it, which i think is a needlesly repressive approach and a very ineffectual way to deal with the matter. Saying that this book "crosses the line" on this matter... Well, to be honest i would probably (although unlikely) agree with you in that, i wouldn't call for it's prohibition on a general but i'm not sure of how ok i would be with it. I would agree that Pwhodges's response is a very reasonable one even if i might not completely agree with it, but appeals to majority ("everybody that I know would give you" "it's the opinion of almost everybody I'd say", last one is particularly funny because it seems based on the assumption that what most people think is the most natural or intelligent opinion, which i feel is kinda divorced from reality.) are not going to work here, or at least i hope so. I have to admit however, that i am confused: In which way would Anno be contradicting himself by making doujinshi? As far as i know he isn't anti-porn. And in the case this might be brought, please note that i roll my eyes at EGF's assertion that one of NGE's main purposes was an attempt of denouncing the otaku culture. I understand that you're referring only to these alleged "extreme cases" (i have seen far worse stuff out there, and not from the internet) but again, for me the principle is the same. I am not familiar or knowledgeable about the Japanese goverment and it's history so i can't say i know why you feel it is horrible, although i would probably agree. However, hating them for not doing something agaisnt doujinshis is to me a very naive and yes, a very arrogant view. Why is it arrogant? Because you intend to force the goverment to apply your viewpoint or judgement into the whole population. I would probably accept that from Barret or some such but i'm not going to accept it from a 17 year old kid. I don't doubt that you have the best intentions when you're calling for it's prohibition but i fear that you haven't really thought it throught. There are many people you would negatively affect if you were able to carry this forth, and the vast majority isn't the kind of people you have in mind when you think of these "extreme cases". I'm not telling you that you should hide away from what you say, just that you should be prepared to accept that if you spew an opinion (specially one along the lines of prohibition or some sort of authoritative thinking) there's a non-trivial probability that people will call you on it, specially if they find your arguments to be poorly formed. Anyways, i apologize for having gotten so worked up and carrying this thread so far off-topic. I think i have said most of what i feel needed to be said so it shouldn't be too hard for me to refrain from posting anymore in this thread. I also hope we get to someday read whatever Anno wrote in there even if it's probably nothing too interesting, at least compared to other scans.

[]:             Quote Post by Caliburn » Wed Jul 10, 2013 4:50 pm Strong subject matter brings with it strong feelings and strong debate. That's normal and I dare say healthy for the discussion, provided people stay polite to each other. Dream, there's no doubt your own passionate feelings showed through in your posts, and perhaps from that point of view you can see why kuribo-04's passionate feelings also showed through in his original post. Don't get me wrong, it's perfectly good to debate the issue at hand, pick at people's rationale, and so on. And perhaps kuribo shouldn't have said "Fuck the Japanese government," etc. But I feel it also wasn't necessary to talk to kuribo-04 in the third-person as if he wasn't there: Dream wrote: Communication of my absolute disgust and comptent for OP and this thread aside ... I'm going to try to ignore OP's arrogance in implying he knows better than Anno and Japan (those wacky 17 years old) ... But it seems that the fact the kids are underage is what bothers our silly OP.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Jul 10, 2013 7:48 pm Mr. Tines, I had heard about that before, and it is something that has to be taken into consideration. Dream. I must apologize. I was a bit arrogant in the first commet, but this is just a topic that really bugs me. Also I don't want you to feel bad, but I want to avoid any kind of fight. So that's why I don't know why you began using words such as silly. If I had the power to decide if this could be prohibited I would obviously discuss it with many people, I don't force my opinion on others, but in a topic like this one the correct answer is pretty obvious. I like Anno, and knowing that he makes child porn would bug me, but that's it, I wouldn't call that delusional. It would just change the opinion I have about him. And I would still consider NGE a great work. Maybe I would enjoy it less, but that is a logical response. But no, I still like him. So that's it. This wasn't really a fight or anything, but pissing of other people makes me nervous, and I just thought that everyone would agree. I've learned a lesson. I now know that people can get angry if something is worded in an arrogant way. I'll keep it in mind. But still, everything I wrote is what I sincerily felt. If you observe other posts of me you will see that I never get into fights and never want to make anyone angry, so believe me if I say that this time it was the same. EDIT: Dream, when I said that part about teenagers, I just meant some fan service elements like the ones in Eva, or a sex scene that could be done with style if necessary for the plot. I just wrote everything very fast. But if it is a comic like the one I was talking about I would not aprove. That was my fault for not wording correctly. Just ignore that part. EDIT2: Thanks Caliburn for your kind approach. EDIT3: Dream, I know that the assumption that Anno just made Evangelion to piss of otakus is pretty wrong. Still he has said that he wanted otakus to grow up, but that now with the Rebuild he doesn't care anymore.

Article: Evangelibon artbook
[]: Evangelibon artbook             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Jul 09, 2013 4:39 pm http://avocado-slice.tumblr.com/post/54976266063/because-i-have-seen-the-comic-angel-kiss-going Released only a month after the series' premiere. Content: An interview with Anno, that I would really like to read, but there is no scan in the internet, in the last pages there are some genga (only four or five I think) and the rest of the book is the worst thing that has ever happened to Evangelion. This is basically hentai drawn by people that participated in the series (Kazuya Tsurumaki is credited, I think). I'm OK with the fan-service in the series, why not? In fact, I couldn't imagine Eva without it. I'm OK with some doujinshi that have been made, even in this book there seem to be some comics that aren't to be taken seriously. But the majority of the book: porn involving 14 year olds. That's it. Fuck the Japanese government for not taking any actions against this type of product, and fuck Gainax for releasing it and shitting all over their best anime in the process. And fuck Anno. Yes, I love Anno, but still, fuck him for not caring what they do with his characters. I don't care what any of the people that worked on this book do, their work for me is like the work of any other otaku on the internet. What bugs me is that Anno (apparently) didn't care. That's why I would like to read the interview. I don't know if this is related to the japaneses' way of thinking, but honestly, I don't care. If sex is is treated like something completely normal, awesome. But that has nothing to do with selling this kind of shit. I repeat, this is porn involving teenagers (I don't care if they are drawn), not made by fans, no, it is made by Gainax. This is kind of ruining Evangelion for me today. EDIT: Oh, and all that talk about making Rei a character that was supposed to shock otakus, this book shits on this too, by including a comic in which she is raped and she doesn't respond in any way. EDIT2: This artbook was made by Gainax staff, but was not published by Gainax

 intelligent opinion, which i feel is kinda divorced from reality.) are not going to work here, or at least i hope so. And in the case this might be brought, please note that i roll my eyes at EGF's assertion that one of NGE's main purposes was an attempt of denouncing the otaku culture. I am not familiar or knowledgeable about the Japanese goverment and it's history so i can't say i know why you feel it is horrible, although i would probably agree. However, hating them for not doing something agaisnt doujinshis is to me a very naive and yes, a very arrogant view. Why is it arrogant? Because you intend to force the goverment to apply your viewpoint or judgement into the whole population. I would probably accept that from Barret or some such but i'm not going to accept it from a 17 year old kid. I don't doubt that you have the best intentions when you're calling for it's prohibition but i fear that you haven't really thought it throught. There are many people you would negatively affect if you were able to carry this forth, and the vast majority isn't the kind of people you have in mind when you think of these "extreme cases". I'm not telling you that you should hide away from what you say, just that you should be prepared to accept that if you spew an opinion (specially one along the lines of prohibition or some sort of authoritative thinking) there's a non-trivial probability that people will call you on it, specially if they find your arguments to be poorly formed. Anyways, i apologize for having gotten so worked up and carrying this thread so far off-topic. I think i have said most of what i feel needed to be said so it shouldn't be too hard for me to refrain from posting anymore in this thread.
